23.reason: [Passing num+1 to left child even when p->left is NULL leads to dereferencing NULL pointer]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Passing NULL pointer to recursive call when p->left is NULL, leading to dereference of NULL in next call]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Passing num+1 to left child even when p->left is NULL leads to dereferencing NULL pointer]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If p->left is NULL, calling search(p->left, ...) will dereference a NULL pointer, causing a runtime crash. The condition should check each child before recursing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If p->left is NULL, calling search with NULL leads to dereferencing NULL in the next recursive call, causing undefined behavior or crash.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If p->left is NULL, calling search(p->left, num+1) will pass NULL to the next recursive call, which then tries to access p->left or p->right on a NULL pointer, causing a segmentation fault. The condition should check for non-NULL before recursing.]

24.reason: [Passing num+1 to right child even when p->right is NULL leads to dereferencing NULL pointer]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Passing NULL pointer to recursive call when p->right is NULL, leading to dereference of NULL in next call]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Passing num+1 to right child even when p->right is NULL leads to dereferencing NULL pointer]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: if p->right is NULL, this call will crash. Recursion should only happen if the child exists.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If p->right is NULL, same issue as above. The condition checks if either child exists, but then unconditionally recurses on both children without checking if they are NULL.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: if p->right is NULL, this call passes NULL into search, leading to dereferencing a NULL pointer in the next call.]

38.reason: [If root is NULL (n=0), this passes NULL to search, which immediately dereferences it]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If root is NULL (n=0), this passes NULL to search, which immediately dereferences it]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If the input n is 0, root will be NULL, and passing NULL to search will cause a crash when accessing p->left or p->right. The program lacks a check for empty tree.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If the tree is empty (n == 0), root is NULL, and passing NULL to search will cause a segmentation fault when accessing p->left or p->right.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If the input n is 0, root remains NULL, and calling search(root,1) will pass NULL to search, which immediately tries to access p->left and p->right, causing a segmentation fault. The program should handle empty tree case.]
